title: WOS Due Process Config
description: A WOS Due Process Config sidecar document. Provides detailed due process configuration -- notice templates, explanation templates, appeal routing, continuation-of-service policies -- for a WOS Workflow Governance Document. Operational parameters can be updated independently of the governance structure.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosDueProcess.description: Document type marker identifying this as a WOS Due Process Config sidecar document (Governance S3). MUST be the string '1.0'. Processors use this field to dispatch to the due process config parser; its absence means the document is not a recognized Due Process Config.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker for WOS Due Process Config sidecar

targetGovernance.description: URI of the WOS Workflow Governance Document this Due Process Config targets. Must match the governance document's url field exactly. Processors MUST reject a Due Process Config whose targetGovernance does not match the governance document being loaded (Governance S3.1).

x-lm.intent: Binds this sidecar to its parent governance document

version.description: Semantic version of this Due Process Config document. Useful for audit trails — allows governance documents to pin a specific due process configuration.

explanationTemplates.description: Templates for decision explanations at various levels.

continuationPolicies.description: Continuation-of-service policies for different adverse decision types.

id.description: Unique identifier for this explanation template within the Due Process Config. Referenced by explanation generation logic in the processor.

level.description: Explanation level this template serves (Governance S3.3). 'individualized': REQUIRED for rights-impacting decisions. 'categorical': RECOMMENDED for operational decisions. 'aggregate': minimum for informational decisions.

items.description: Named element that MUST be present in explanations generated from this template.

requiredElements.description: Named elements that MUST be present in explanations generated from this template. Element names are workflow-defined; the processor validates presence in the rendered explanation.

description.description: Human-readable description of this explanation template's purpose and scope. Intended for governance authors and auditors.
examples: Individualized explanation for adverse benefits decisions — includes counterfactuals and appeal rights per APA requirements.
Categorical explanation for routine denials — summarizes applicable rule categories without individual counterfactuals.

defaultReviewerPool.description: Default pool of actors eligible to review appeals when no specific reviewer is assigned. Referenced by the processor during appeal routing.

independenceConstraint.description: How independence from the original determination is ensured for appeal reviewers (Governance S3.5). Processors MUST enforce this constraint during reviewer assignment.

items.description: Actor pool or role identifier representing one escalation level in the appeal review chain.

escalationPath.description: Ordered escalation path for contested appeal reviews. If an appeal reviewer's decision is contested, the processor routes to the next level in this sequence.

id.description: Unique identifier for this continuation policy within the Due Process Config. Referenced by `AppealMechanism.continuationPolicyRef` in the parent governance document so an appeal that turns on `continuationOfServices: true` resolves to this exact policy (Governance §3.6).

x-lm.intent: Identifier referenced by AppealMechanism.continuationPolicyRef to bind an appeal to a specific continuation-of-service policy

decisionType.description: Type of adverse decision this continuation-of-service policy applies to. Matches the workflow's adverse decision classification vocabulary.

scope.description: Scope of services maintained during the appeal window under this policy. Determines what the individual continues to receive while their appeal is pending.

duration.description: ISO 8601 duration for which services are continued pending appeal resolution. When the appeal window expires without resolution, the processor applies the configured timeout action.

description.description: Human-readable description of this continuation-of-service policy and its regulatory basis. Intended for governance authors and auditors.
examples: Benefit termination: current-level continuation for 30 days pending appeal, per APA 5 USC 554.
Benefit reduction: continue at current level for 14 days to allow appeal filing.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosKernel': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
